celebration: THE TWO-SYLLABLE GATE OPENS — for the first time the class reads a word in two beats (un·der, bet·ter, sis·ter, teach·er). One sound, three spellings: the er/ir/ur leaf goes up on the right branch of the Pattern Tree.

songs.title: Er-Er-Bird!
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
(er… er… er…)
One sound! Three ways!

[Hook]
Er! Er! Er-er-er!
Spell it e-r… i-r… u-r… ONE sound!
Er-er-bird! Er-er-bird!
It's a bird! (a bird!)

[Verse 2]
Er! Er! Er-er-er!
Her! Fur! Turn!
Er-er-girl! Er-er-girl!
It's a girl! (a girl!)

[Verse 3]
Er! Er! Er-er-er!
under… better… sister…
Two beats, ONE word!
It's a teacher! (hello, teacher!)

[Bridge]
A potato?! (NO!)
P-p-potato! Not er!
(er… er… er…)

[Outro]
e-r bird… i-r girl… u-r fur…
One sound! (bird!)
(er… shhh…)
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: er is the r-controlled growl /ɜː/ — a low held vowel, NEVER the letter names 'ee-arr'. The whole lesson is 'one sound, three spellings' — e-r, i-r, u-r are said as uppercase letter names (whole-words rule; never syllable-split a word). Verse 3 is the TWO-SYLLABLE GATE: sing 'under / better / sister' as whole words on two beats (two 808 hits), then land 'teacher'. Potato bridge intact.

songs.title: The Bird and the Girl
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
(We can read. We can read!)

[Hook]
The bird and the girl!
The bird and the girl!
Where is the bird? (where? where?)
Under the fern!

[Verse 2]
The bird is hurt. (poor bird…)
Her name is Segina.
Soft is the fur! (soft! soft!)
Warm is the bird.

[Verse 3]
The bird can turn! (turn! turn!)
Better and better!
Where is the bird? (where? where?)
Up! Up! It can fly!

[Outro]
The fern… the bird… (shhh…)
What is under the fern?!
(POTATO!)
The bird and the girl. (the end.)
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: Frame: 'The bird and the girl.' — a gentle rescue story, Segina and a hurt bird. Sing 'better and better' as whole words (the two-syllable gate, said warmly). The 'Where is the ___?' call-and-response carries over from Level 1. Whisper the outro, then the potato hits loud under the fern.

spreads.artNote: A tiny bird perched on a curled green fern frond in the spotlight. New sound, same dark theatre.
The little bird on the ground, one wing drooping, looking up. Tender, not grim.
Segina — the peg-doll girl with black pigtails and a red dress — kneeling into the light, hands cupped. 'Her' is one of the week's new words.
Segina reaching gently toward the bird, warm and careful.

spreads.text: The bird is under her shirt.
spreads.artNote: The bird tucked warm under Segina's collar, only its head peeking out. The two-syllable word 'under' printed for the first time. (Text note: 'she' and 'put' are untaught heart words — never print them; Segina's name carries the sentence.)

spreads.text: The fur is soft and warm.
spreads.artNote: Close-up: the soft fur trim of Segina's collar around the resting bird, everything golden and warm.
The bird lifting its head, turning to look at Segina — the first sign it is mending.
The bird up on Segina's finger, wings spread, ready to fly. 'Better' — the two-syllable word — is the triumph.
The bird has flown; where it sat under the fern, a single potato now rests wearing a tiny fern-leaf crown. No noun printed — the class SHOUTS it. Segina blinks.

sentences: She put the bird under her shirt.
extras: THE TWO-SYLLABLE GATE: this is the week two-part words become readable. Present the 'clap-and-blend' box — under, better, sister, teacher, farmer, winter — a card cut between the syllables (un|der). The child blends part one, part two, then reads the whole word. First time a Montree child reads a two-beat word.

ONE SOUND, THREE SPELLINGS: the er/ir/ur sort. Three columns (her/fern · bird/girl · fur/turn). The children discover the SAME growl /ɜː/ hides behind three different spellings — the first 'spelling choice' of the program.
Pattern Tree: the class reads her/bird/fur/turn aloud and hangs the er/ir/ur leaf — the first leaf on the RIGHT branch (Level 3 begins to fill).
Moveable alphabet: build h-er, b-ir-d, f-ur, t-ur-n, then the two-syllable words un-der, sis-ter with a small gap, closing the gap as they read.

teacherFocus: THE TWO-SYLLABLE GATE. Until now every word was one beat; today two-part words open up — under, better, sister, teacher, farmer, winter. Present the clap-and-blend box (a card cut between the syllables, un|der) and let them close the gap. Then the sort that defines the week: ONE growl /ɜː/, THREE spellings (er, ir, ur) — the first time a Montree child must CHOOSE a spelling. Don't drill the choice yet; just let them notice her/bird/fur all growl the same. Hang the first right-branch leaf with ceremony.
